original_hypothesis.title: PairFactory setGenesisStatus allows bricking uninitialized pairs
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The setGenesisStatus function lacks an isPair validation check, allowing the genesisManager to set genesis status for uncreated pair addresses, permanently preventing their initialization.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Unlike setIsGenesis, setGenesisStatus does not check require(isPair[_pairAddress]). The genesisManager can set isGenesis = true for an address that has not yet been deployed as a pair. When the pair is eventually created, RouterV2.addLiquidity will revert because it checks require(!(isGenesis(pair) && totalSupply == 0)). This permanently prevents the pair from receiving initial liquidity.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing isPair validation check present in similar functions

preconditions: genesisManager role is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: State mapping updated for arbitrary addresses
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Griefing attack to prevent competitors from launching pools

economic_questions: Can pair addresses be deterministically predicted before creation?
validation_requirements: Predict pair address, set genesis status to true
Create pair and attempt to add initial liquidity

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Before a highly anticipated token launch
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Prevents new markets from forming on the protocol

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Token projects attempting to launch pools

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
